what_it_does: Acceptance test for G6 voice coupling. It drives runtime.bridge._stream_parts with stub generators and a live local bridge to prove that brain generation and TTS synthesis can overlap per part, that chunk indices stay monotonic across parts, that cancellation stops the next part from being generated, that single-part and off/refusal paths still work, that producer exceptions are re-raised loudly, and that existing UX contracts (one chat event via chat_parts, iOS unlock untouched, speak() signature, trial recording once per turn) are preserved.
observations: Deterministic overlap proof uses a paced two-part generator and stub speak/emit functions with real timestamps.
Cancellation test gates part generation with a threading.Event so the assertion is deterministic rather than timing-racy.
The test inspects runtime/bridge.py source text to enforce structural contracts such as calling SUITE.chat_parts and not SUITE.chat.
A live by-use section POSTs a synthesized clip to /api/voice/stream on :8772 and is skipped-with-note if the bridge is unreachable.
An authoritative in-process PRESERVE-4 section drives the real _stream_parts over a real Suite.chat_parts generator in an isolated FsStore.
The file writes evidence JSON artifacts to /tmp/g6_overlap_timeline.json and /tmp/g6_live_stream_events.json.
It uses a global PASS counter and ok flag with a check() helper rather than a framework assertion style.
standouts: C6.1 deterministic, model-speed-independent proof of brain↔TTS overlap using injected stubs and timestamped events.
PRESERVE-4 authoritative in-process assertion that a multi-part turn adds exactly two chat-history rows (one user + one assistant).
Structural source-code assertions on _voice_stream to lock in single-chat-event, iOS-unlock, speak-signature, and trial-recording contracts.
conventions: Acceptance test organized into numbered sections with explicit C6.x / PRESERVE labels.
Dependency injection into _stream_parts via speak_fn, emit_fn, gone, and split_sentences parameters.
Timing assertions use time.monotonic and real sleeps to observe concurrency.
Live integration checks degrade to skipped-with-note when the bridge or engine is unavailable.
Debug evidence is persisted to /tmp JSON files.
concerns: Hard-coded /tmp paths and fixed sleeps (0.6s, 0.05s) make the overlap test timing-sensitive and environment-dependent.
Source-code string inspection of bridge.py is brittle to formatting or import-order changes.
Global mutable PASS/ok state and many nested local functions reduce test isolation.
The live by-use section is long and mixes HTTP, TTS, STT, chat history, and daemon-thread races, making failures harder to attribute.
Some assertions depend on exact chunk counts produced by the local _split regex.
notes: A dense acceptance harness that tries to prove voice streaming overlaps brain generation without regressing existing frontend/backend contracts.
questions: What is the exact contract and return shape of runtime.bridge._stream_parts?
How does the chat_parts generator signal final result versus early_return, and what fields does each yielded part dict carry?
What are the precise semantics of the gone flag and the optional should_stop callback?
Is the live bridge on :8772 expected to run in CI, or is the by-use section strictly local/manual?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it tests G6 voice-coupling overlap and preservation invariants as described in the module docstring.
apparent_themes: Deterministic acceptance testing through stub injection
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish that voice streaming can synthesize each part while the next part is still being generated, while keeping all surrounding seams (chat, speak, trial recording, iOS unlock) unchanged.
novelty: A model-speed-independent deterministic overlap proof that timestamps stub synthesis chunks against a deliberately slowed second-part generator.
contribution: A concrete acceptance contract for _stream_parts that documents expected overlap, monotonic chunk indexing, cancellation gating, single-part brevity, exception propagation, and preservation of chat/speak/trial-recording behavior.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for G6 voice coupling that exercises runtime.bridge._stream_parts with stub and live generators to prove brain↔TTS overlap, monotonic chunk indexing, cancellation gating, single-part brevity, loud exceptions, and preservation of chat/speak/trial-recording contracts.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to validate that part-based voice streaming overlaps brain generation without breaking existing frontend and backend contracts.

symbols.does: Global pass/fail counter and reporter for acceptance checks.
symbols.description: Increments PASS and prints PASS when cond is true; otherwise sets ok=False and prints FAIL. It is the only assertion mechanism used in the file.

symbols.does: Splits text into sentences for the acceptance tests.
symbols.description: Uses a regex split on sentence-ending punctuation to break a string into sentence-sized chunks, falling back to the whole text if no split is found.

symbols.does: Stub generator that yields two parts with a delay before part 2.
symbols.description: Part 1 is produced immediately; part 2 is delayed by 0.6 seconds so the test can observe part-1 synthesis chunks emitted before part 2 has even been generated.

symbols.does: Fake TTS function that returns WAV bytes after a small delay.
symbols.description: Simulates speech synthesis by sleeping 0.05 seconds and returning b"WAVDATA:" plus the input sentence encoded as bytes.

symbols.does: Records each emitted chunk with a timestamp.
symbols.description: Appends a tuple of chunk index, text, and elapsed time to chunk_times and logs the event for the overlap timeline.

symbols.does: Three-part generator gated by an event to test cancellation.
symbols.description: Yields part 1, then waits on gone_is_set before generating part 2, and would yield part 3 only if cancellation did not stop the stream.

symbols.does: Stub speak that triggers client cancellation during the first synthesis.
symbols.description: Sets gone[0]=True and signals gone_is_set during the first speak call, allowing the test to verify that part 3 is never generated.

symbols.does: Generator yielding a single final part for the brevity path.
symbols.description: Produces one part with final=True and staged=False, exercising the single-part bypass without overlap machinery.
Produces a single part whose reply is read from early_return rather than result, testing the refusal/off path.

symbols.does: Generator that raises an exception after the first part.
symbols.description: Yields part 1 successfully, then raises RuntimeError to verify that producer-thread exceptions are re-raised on the handler thread.

symbols.does: Probes the live bridge health endpoint.
symbols.description: Performs a GET to /api/now on the configured BRIDGE URL and returns True only if the response status is 200.

symbols.does: Counts chat events from the bridge event log.
symbols.description: Fetches /api/events and returns the number of events whose kind equals "chat", or None if the request fails.

symbols.does: Returns the length of the bridge chat history.
symbols.description: Fetches /api/chat and returns the length of the returned array, or None if the request fails.

symbols.does: Sentence splitter used by the authoritative PRESERVE-4 test.
symbols.description: Same regex-based sentence split as _split but defined locally for the in-process Suite.chat_parts deterministic check.
